title: corynocarpus dissimilis
intro: Corynocarpus dissimilis, also known as the New Zealand laurel or karaka tree, is a native plant species found in New Zealand. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its seeds, which contain a high concentration of an oil called coriariin. This oil has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, and its health benefits are attributed to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Corynocarpus dissimilis seeds can be prepared into herbal remedies, such as tinctures and infusions, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses.
